data.name: Andre
data.title: Fullstack Software Engineer & DevOps Apprentice
data.summary: Software Engineer and Platform Engineering practitioner with a Bac+5 RNCP Level 7 — building production systems that are secure, observable, and AI-native. Author of the minicloud platform: a 67-phase enterprise Kubernetes project running 54+ production workloads, 9/9 OPA Gatekeeper admission policies in deny mode, and a full AI Gateway with RAG, LLMOps, and 62 automated regression checks. Currently an IT Automation Engineer Apprentice at HDI Global SE France, applying the same engineering rigour to real enterprise workflows.

projects.title: Minicloud Platform
projects.purpose: Production-grade enterprise Kubernetes platform built on 4 bare-metal ThinkPad laptops to demonstrate the full private-cloud engineering lifecycle. Deployed 54+ workloads via ArgoCD GitOps: Vault secrets management, Authentik SSO/OIDC, Harbor private registry, Falco runtime security, Prometheus/Grafana/Loki observability stack, and an enterprise AI Gateway (LiteLLM + Ollama) with multi-provider routing, PII/DLP guardrails, and a full RAG pipeline. Hardened with zero-trust NetworkPolicy across all 23 namespaces and supply-chain security (cosign, SBOM, GPG-signed commits).
projects.role: Platform Engineer & Solutions Architect. Designed the enterprise GitOps repository structure (app-of-apps pattern, Kustomize Combo 1 base+overlays, 3-branch CI promotion flow, AppProject with locked sourceRepos and clusterResourceWhitelist). Built the zero-trust network topology, the AI Gateway with 8 cloud/local providers and Presidio PII guardrails, the RAG pipeline (bge-m3 + pgvector HNSW + hybrid BM25 + cross-encoder re-ranking + Docling OCR), and automated 62 regression checks covering the full platform.
projects.impact: 62 automated regression checks continuously green. 9/9 OPA Gatekeeper admission policies in deny mode with 0 violations. Zero-trust NetworkPolicy enforced across all 23 namespaces. Vault auto-unseal via AWS KMS. Enterprise RAG with sub-400ms embedding latency and <1ms HNSW vector scan. AI Gateway routing 7 providers with circuit-breaker, per-department key governance, and Langfuse LLMOps tracing.
features: Enterprise GitOps: app-of-apps ArgoCD pattern, Kustomize base+overlays (dev/staging/prod), CI promotes via kustomize edit set image to dev only — PR-gated staging and prod, AppProject with locked sourceRepos and explicit namespace/resource whitelist
9/9 OPA Gatekeeper admission policies — deny mode, 0 violations
Zero-trust NetworkPolicy across 23 Kubernetes namespaces
AI Gateway: 8 providers, PII/DLP guardrails, cloud fallback chain, Valkey cache
Enterprise RAG: bge-m3 + pgvector HNSW + BM25 hybrid search + Docling OCR
Full supply chain: cosign signing, SBOM, GPG commits, branch protection, Dependabot

projects.title: phi3-financial — Enterprise PromptOps Pipeline
projects.purpose: Domain-restricted AI financial assistant with a production PromptOps architecture. Built a LiteLLM CustomLogger (LangfusePromptHandler) that fetches the active production-labelled prompt from Langfuse at request time with 5-minute in-process cache and a three-layer fail-open chain — enabling instant prompt rollbacks without code deploys or image rebuilds. Includes a 25-case CI eval suite enforcing 100% pass rate as a deployment gate.
projects.role: AI Engineer. Designed the three-tier architecture (Ollama model serving → LiteLLM routing + runtime prompt injection → Langfuse LLMOps), built the async_pre_call_hook CustomLogger, wrote the 25-case eval suite across 5 behavioral categories, and implemented semantic versioning with automatic git tagging on every passing CI run.
projects.impact: 25/25 eval cases passing (100% — deployment blocked on any regression). Prompt rollback time: under 5 minutes via Langfuse label change — no git push, no pod restart required. Root-caused a 90-second CI outage (ArgoCD Recreate restart overlapping eval runs) and fixed at two layers: RollingUpdate strategy + exponential retry logic in the eval harness.
features: LangfusePromptHandler: runtime prompt injection via LiteLLM CustomLogger (async_pre_call_hook)
25-case eval suite: financial Q&A, injection resistance, off-topic refusal, mixed-domain, market manipulation
Semantic versioning (MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH) + CI auto git-tagging on 100% pass
Fail-open chain: Langfuse → stale cache → ConfigMap env var — never blocks requests
Weekly scheduled CI eval + deployment gate enforced via GitHub Actions

projects.title: Enterprise RAG Pipeline
projects.purpose: Built a full multilingual RAG pipeline for French insurance document retrieval across three custom-built repos. minicloud-markitdown-proxy (FastAPI): routes documents by type — PDF/images to Docling OCR, Office/HTML to MarkItDown in-pod — exposing a Docling-compatible API surface. minicloud-rag-ingest (FastAPI): full ingestion pipeline in one HTTP endpoint — convert → structure-aware heading chunk (French insurance regex) → bge-m3 1024-dim embed → pgvector HNSW INSERT. minicloud-postgresql-noavx512: custom PostgreSQL image with pgvector's vector.so rebuilt without -mavx512f/bw/vl/dq to fix a SIGILL crash on i7-8565U/i7-10510U CPUs caused by AVX-512 EVEX instructions in pgvector 0.8.4. Hybrid search layer: BM25 with French Snowball stemmer + NLTK stop-words injected via init container patching rank_bm25/utils.py at pod start. Cross-encoder re-ranking (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2) filters to top-3.
projects.role: Systems Engineer & ML Engineer. Diagnosed the SIGILL root cause (pgvector 0.8.4 HNSW code emitting AVX-512 EVEX instructions not supported by i7-8565U/i7-10510U), rebuilt vector.so from source with AVX-512 disabled, and packaged it as a custom Harbor image. Designed the two-microservice ingestion architecture, wrote the French BM25 preprocessor (NLTK Snowball stemmer + hardcoded insurance stop-word set patched into rank_bm25/utils.py via init container subPath mount), and wired Docling as the PDF/OCR backend. Both microservices shipped with Dockerfile, GitHub Actions CI, and cosign keyless signing.
projects.impact: Unblocked all HNSW index operations on CPUs without AVX-512 — zero SIGILL crashes post-fix. Measured pipeline latencies: HNSW vector scan 0.31ms, BM25 GIN FTS 2.71ms, INSERT + HNSW update 6.8ms/row, bge-m3 embedding 393ms. French stemming verified: sinistres/sinistre → sinistr ✅, réassurance/réassurer → réassur ✅. Full end-to-end in-cluster: PDF upload → Docling OCR → heading-aware chunks → 1024-dim vectors → pgvector HNSW + BM25 hybrid → cross-encoder top-3. No external API calls.
features: minicloud-markitdown-proxy: PDF/images → Docling OCR, Office/HTML → MarkItDown in-pod (Docling-compatible API)
minicloud-rag-ingest: single HTTP endpoint — convert → chunk → bge-m3 embed → pgvector HNSW INSERT
minicloud-postgresql-noavx512: vector.so rebuilt without AVX-512 — fixed SIGILL on i7-8565U/i7-10510U (pgvector 0.8.4 SIMD bug)
French BM25: NLTK Snowball stemmer + insurance stop-words patched into rank_bm25 via Kubernetes init container
Hybrid retrieval: bge-m3 1024-dim vector + BM25 + cross-encoder re-ranking (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, top-3)
Supply chain: Dockerfile + GitHub Actions CI + cosign keyless signing on both microservices
Generation step routed through Enterprise AI Gateway (LiteLLM) — RAG retrieval + cloud/local model selection in one pipeline
stack: PostgreSQL + pgvector (custom noavx512 build, C / Makefile)

projects.title: Enterprise AI Gateway
projects.purpose: Multi-provider LLM routing and enterprise governance platform built on LiteLLM on Kubernetes. Unified 8 cloud and local providers (Groq, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Mistral, Anthropic Claude, HuggingFace featherless-ai via router.huggingface.co, and local Ollama) behind a single API endpoint with intelligent fallback routing (Ollama→Groq→DeepSeek), Presidio PII/DLP scrubbing, detect_secrets credential scanning, 3-tier department key governance with per-dept budget caps and model allowlists, circuit breaker protection, Valkey semantic cache, and full Langfuse LLMOps tracing. Cost visibility delivered via a Grafana dashboard reading LiteLLM's PostgreSQL usage tables. Includes Langfuse v3.201.1 with ClickHouse analytics backend and Authentik OIDC SSO, plus an NVIDIA NIM tier (nemotron-70b, llama-8b, deepseek-r1).
projects.role: AI Platform Engineer. Designed the 8-provider routing topology and 3-stage fallback chain, implemented Presidio PII and detect_secrets credential scanning as LiteLLM hook layers, built the 3-tier department key governance model with per-dept budget caps and model allowlists, configured the circuit breaker (cooldown_time=60s, allowed_fails=3), deployed Langfuse v3.201.1 with ClickHouse+Valkey and wired Authentik OIDC for SSO access, and built the Grafana cost dashboard with SQL queries against LiteLLM's PostgreSQL usage tables.
projects.impact: 8 LLM providers unified behind one endpoint — cloud or local, transparently. deepseek-r1:7b cloud-first cuts inference from 40+s (local CPU) to 5-8s (DeepSeek cloud API). Automatic failover: applications survive a provider outage with zero code changes. Department budget caps enforce FinOps boundaries at the API layer. Every request traced in Langfuse with cost, latency, and model metadata for full audit.
features: 8-provider routing: Groq, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Mistral, Anthropic Claude, HuggingFace featherless-ai, Ollama — single LiteLLM endpoint
Intelligent fallback chain: Ollama → Groq → DeepSeek — automatic failover on provider unavailability
Presidio PII/DLP + detect_secrets guardrails — privacy and credential protection on every request
3-tier department key governance: per-dept budget caps, model allowlists, and usage quotas
Circuit breaker (cooldown=60s, allowed_fails=3) + Valkey semantic cache — resilience and cost optimisation
Langfuse LLMOps (v3.201.1 + ClickHouse + Valkey + Authentik OIDC) + Grafana cost dashboard via PostgreSQL SQL

projects.title: Enterprise AI Chat Platform
projects.purpose: Deployed Open WebUI as a production enterprise AI chat interface serving 16 business departments through Authentik OIDC SSO. Connects the full AI backend stack — LiteLLM AI Gateway (8 providers), RAG pipeline (French insurance knowledge base), and SearXNG real-time web search — into a single product accessible to non-technical business users. phi3-financial serves as the default model for finance analysts, domain-restricted and prompt-governed at runtime via Langfuse. Conversation history and user management backed by PostgreSQL for production-grade persistence.
projects.role: Platform & AI Product Engineer. Wired Authentik OIDC into Open WebUI, resolving SSL verification failure against the self-signed cluster CA via an init container that concatenates system CAs and the minicloud CA into a single bundle (SSL_CERT_FILE=/ca-bundle/bundle.crt). Deployed SearXNG with two non-obvious gotchas: enableServiceLinks=false to prevent Kubernetes injecting a SEARXNG_PORT env var that crashes granian, and BYPASS_WEB_SEARCH_WEB_LOADER=true for news site scraping compatibility. Migrated conversation storage from SQLite to PostgreSQL and wired the AI Gateway and RAG pipeline as backends.
projects.impact: 16 business departments with SSO-controlled AI access — each user sees only their own conversation history. Finance analysts get phi3-financial by default: domain-restricted, prompt-rolled-back in under 5 minutes via Langfuse. Users query a French insurance document knowledge base directly in chat via the RAG pipeline. Real-time web search via self-hosted SearXNG. Zero conversation data loss on pod restarts — PostgreSQL-backed.
features: Authentik OIDC SSO — 16 department groups, per-dept model defaults, conversation isolation enforced by design
phi3-financial default for finance — domain-restricted, runtime prompt injection via Langfuse (instant rollback, no redeploy)
Enterprise RAG in chat — French insurance knowledge base queryable via bge-m3 hybrid search + cross-encoder re-ranking
SearXNG real-time web search — self-hosted meta-search, no tracking, globe icon in chat UI
PostgreSQL conversation persistence — production-grade, migrated from SQLite, zero data loss on pod restart
SSL CA bundle init container — solved OIDC token/JWKS SSL verification failure against self-signed cluster CA

workExperience.title: IT Operations & Automation Engineer (Apprentice)
workExperience.subtitle: HDI Global SE France, Paris
workExperience.description:
• Automation: Built batch scripting workflows for XML data import generation → reduced manual processing time by 70% (Python, Batch).
  • IAM & Workflow: Digitalized IT access request workflows using SharePoint and Power Automate, covering 200+ employees.
  • Patch Management: Supported monthly patch cycles and non-regression testing (ALM Octane) across production environments.
  • ITSM: Handled N1/N2 support via ServiceNow and GLPI; coordinated renewal of ~200 workstations.
  • RBAC: Restructured role-based access control across enterprise applications, reducing unauthorized access incidents.

  • Frontend Refactor: Refactored UI across a hybrid stack (React, TypeScript, Laravel) and applied rendering and asset optimizations to improve Core Web Vitals scores.
